title: Privacy Policy
content:
Last updated: March 19, 2026

This Policy details how <bold>ft_transcendence</bold> treats personal data, following the guidelines of the General Data Protection Law (LGPD).

<bold>1. Data Collection</bold>
• <bold>Registration Data:</bold> We collect email and basic profile information via Google or GitHub for authentication purposes.
• <bold>Usage Data:</bold> The system processes HTTP requests to validate parameters and record end-user interactions.
• <bold>Tracking Data:</bold> We record clicks and message opens to provide performance analysis to our clients.

<bold>1.2. Purpose of Data Processing</bold>
• Operationalize the sending and personalization of marketing messages.
• Generate reports and performance analysis of campaigns for clients.
• Ensure data security and isolation between different companies.

<bold>2. Data Retention</bold>
• Records of tracking events and user interactions (clicks, opens, sends) will be stored for a period of 1 year.
• After this period, data will be anonymized for statistical purposes or permanently deleted.

<bold>3. International Data Transfer</bold>
• To enable simplified login via Google and GitHub, international transfer of authentication data may occur.
• These transfers are performed only to ensure the secure access functionality of the platform.

<bold>4. Security and Architecture</bold>
• Data is processed through a layered architecture that ensures organization and security in database access.
• Access to raw data is restricted to the backend processes necessary for the execution of business rules.

<bold>5. Contact and Data Protection Officer (DPO)</bold>
To exercise your rights of access, correction, or deletion of data, contact our Data Protection Officer via email: <bold>dpo@ft_transcendence.42.fr</bold>
